entry.title: rap preview with odata v4 works, with odata v2 does not
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I have created a rap service on sap cloud trial account. Originally I've used odata v4. When I want to make preview, it shows all data as expected. When I switch to odata V2, it shows just a blank page. No error noticed in error log so far. Does anybody have similar experience? How to overcome this issue?

Thank you in advance.

Regards,

Radim

entry.title: Requirement for Generating Random Serial numbers in Process Order in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
We have a client requirement to create random serial numbers within a Process Order ( Range: 300000000 to 399999999 )

But system is generating the number serially whereas we need them to be randomized. Like for 10 Units of FG we require 10 random numbers from Range: 300000000 to 399999999

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Requirement for Generating Random Serial numbers in P...
entry.content: In S/4HANA Public Cloud the BADI LOSN_CBO_SERIAL_NUMBER_INT is available where you can extend the serial number creation function to create serial numbers automatically in accordance with a custom pattern.

entry.title: SAP Fiori Elements List Report: Implementing Dual Data Views with Flat and Hierarchical Entities
entry.content:
In many SAP Fiori Elements applications, data is modeled hierarchically. Consider a scenario with a 3-level entity structure:

  • Header (parent)
  • Item (child)
  • SubItem (grandchild)

By default, the List Report displays data at the Header level. However, there is a requirement to provide a Detailed view: a flat representation that surfaces fields from all three levels in a single row, without requiring navigation into each record.

Goal:

Segmented button on the List Report that lets the user toggle between:

  • Detailed View: flat structure, fields from all 3 levels in one row
  • Normal View: standard hierarchical view at the Header level

Why this approach

Instead of modifying the existing hierarchical entity, a separate flat view entity allows both representations to coexist without impacting the original data model.

 

Backend: Exposing a Flat View Entity (CAP)

The standard Header entity only holds parent-level fields. To surface child and grandchild fields in a single flat list, a dedicated view entity is exposed in the CAP service.

This entity (HeaderDetailView) is built using a bottom-to-top approach, starting from SubItem and joining upward through Item to Header, making all fields available at a single level.

// In schema.cds

entity HeaderDetailView as select from SubItem {
    // SubItem fields
    subItemField1 as field1,
    subItemField2 as field2,

    // Item fields (via association)
    item.itemField1 as field3
    item.itemField2 as field4

    // Header fields (via association)
    item.header.headerField1 as field5,
    item.header.headerField2 as field6,
    item.header.ID as ID
};

This entity is then exposed in the service:

// In service.cds

service AppService {
    entity Header         as projection on db.Header;
    entity HeaderDetailView as projection on db.HeaderDetailView;
}

 

UI: Wiring Two Entities into One List Report

The UI layer uses annotations and manifest configuration to expose both entities as separate views within the same List Report.

Step 1: Define LineItem annotations for both views

Detailed view: on HeaderDetailView:

//In Annotations.cds file

annotate service.HeaderDetailView with @(UI: { 
    HeaderInfo                       : {
        TypeName      : 'Item',
        TypeNamePlural: 'Items'
    },
    PresentationVarinat #DetailedView: {
        Visualizations: [{$AnnotationPath:'HeaderDetailView/@UI.LineItem#DetailedView'}],
        GroupBy       : [ID],
    },
    PresentationVarinat #NormalView  : {
        Visualizations: [{$AnnotationPath:'Header/@UI.LineItem'}],
    },
    LineItem #DetailedView           : [
        {
            $Type            : 'UI.DataField',
            Value            : ID,
            ![@UI.Importance]: #High
        },
        {
            $Type            : 'UI.DataField',
            Value            : field1,
            ![@UI.Importance]: #High
        },
        //add other fields in similar fashion
    ],
});

Normal view: on Header:

//In Annotations.cds file

annotate service.Header with @(UI: { 
    HeaderInfo                       : {
        TypeName      : 'Item',
        TypeNamePlural: 'Items'
    },
    LineItem          : [
        {
            $Type            : 'UI.DataField',
            Value            : ID,
            ![@UI.Importance]: #High
        },
        {
            $Type            : 'UI.DataField',
            Value            : field1,
            ![@UI.Importance]: #High
        },
        //add other header level-fields in similar fashion
    ],
});

 

Step 2: Define SelectionPresentationVariant on both entities

This is what the List Report uses to derive the tab label and resolve the correct LineItem.

//In Annotations.cds file

annotate service.HeaderDetailView with @(UI: {
    SelectionPresentationVariant #DetailedView: {
        $Type              : 'UI.SelectionPresentationVariantType',
        Text               : 'Detailed View',
        PresentationVariant: {
            Visualizations: ['@UI.LineItem#DetailedView']
        }
    }
});

annotate service.Header with @(UI: { 
    SelectionPresentationVariant #NormalView: {
        $Type              : 'UI.SelectionPresentationVariantType',
        Text               : 'Normal View',
        PresentationVariant: {
            Visualizations: ['@UI.LineItem']
        }
    }
});

 

Step 3: Configure manifest.json

"targets": {
        "HeaderList": {
          "type": "Component",
          "id": "HeaderList",
          "name": "sap.fe.templates.ListReport",
          "options": {
            "settings": {
              "contextPath": "/HeaderDetailView",
              "views": {
                "paths": [
                  {
                    "key": "tab1",
                    "annotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.SelectionPresentationVariant#DetailedView"
                  },
                  {
                    "key": "tab2",
                    "entitySet": "ObjectPageEntity",
                    "annotationPath": "com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.SelectionPresentationVariant#NormalView"
                  }
                ]

              },
              
              "enhanceI18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
              "variantManagement": "Page",
              "initialLoad": "Enabled",
              "navigation": {
                "ObjectPageEntity": {
                  "detail": {
                    "route": "ObjectPageEntityRoute"
                  }
                }
              },
              "controlConfiguration": {
                "@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.SelectionPresentationVariant#DetailedView": {
                  "tableSettings": {
                    "type": "ResponsiveTable",
                    "disableCopyToClipboard": true,
                    "enablePaste": false,
                    "personalization": {
                      "column": true,
                      "sort": true,
                      "filter": true,
                      "group": true
                    }
                  },
                  "columns": {
                  }
                },
                "/Header/@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.SelectionPresentationVariant#NormalView": {
                  "tableSettings": {
                    "type": "ResponsiveTable",
                    "disableCopyToClipboard": true,
                    "enablePaste": false,
                    "personalization": {
                      "filter": true,
                      "group":false
                    }
                  },
                  "columns": {
                 }
           }
        },

This configuration enables the List Report to render multiple views backed by different entity sets.

Key points:

  • contextPath points to the flat view entity (HeaderDetailView) as the primary entity
  • Tab2 uses entitySet to point to the hierarchical entity (Header)
  • annotationPath must point to SelectionPresentationVariant (not LineItem), as it provides both the visualization reference and the tab label.

Result

The List Report renders a segmented button on the table toolbar, allowing users to switch between Detailed View and Normal View. Thus, both views are available at the same List Report level, without requiring navigation.

>> Normal View:

>> Detailed View:

Summary

Comparison of Views:

 

Detailed View

Normal View

Entity

HeaderDetailView (flat)

Header (hierarchical)

Structure

Fields from all 3 levels

Parent-level fields only

Annotation

UI.LineItem#DetailedView

UI.LineItem

Variant

SelectionPresentationVariant#DetailedView

SelectionPresentationVariant#NormalView

 

Final takeaway

SAP Fiori Elements List Report supports multiple views backed by different entity sets, not just filtered variants of the same entity. SelectionPresentationVariant acts as the bridge between CDS annotations and manifest configuration, controlling both the tab label and the rendered content.

When to use this approach

  • When a hierarchical model needs a flattened analytical view
  • When users need detailed data without navigating to Object Pages
  • When multiple representations of the same data are required in a single List Report

entry.title: Preparation to Migration of New Accrual Engine
entry.content:
Starting with release S/4HANA 1809, the new S/4HANA Accrual Engine becomes available. The programs for the Manual Accruals application have transitioned from the classic Accrual Engine to the S/4HANA Accrual Engine. As a result, you need to migrate the data and customizing settings for Manual Accruals from the old Accrual Engine to the S/4HANA Accrual Engine after upgrading to release S/4HANA 1809. If you've been using Manual Accruals before the upgrade and want to continue using them afterward, this migration is mandatory.  (SAP KBA 3701921)

The engine enables complex postings. You can define posting schemas that allow more than two line items. It also allows optional review and/or approval of periodic accruals at the period end. 

Migration Activities (ACEMIGIMG) 

The data model of customizing and transactional data of the (new) S/4HANA Accrual Engine has changed compared to the old Accrual Engine. Several of the old tables have been replaced by new tables. As a consequence, both types of tables, customizing and transactional data, need to be migrated after the upgrade to S/4HANA 1809 or higher. 

This migration is necessary after an upgrade from: 

  • A ‘classic’ ERP release 
  • An S/4HANA Finance (aka sFIN) release 
  • An S/4HANA release < 1809 

Note: The transactional data of the Accrual Engine consists of: 

  • Accrual Objects 
  • Accrual Postings 

Required migration of transparent tables: 

 

 

 

 

Timing and Independence of Migration 

The migration of Accrual Engine transactional data can be performed after the migration of all other postings in the system (either of the migration tool from ECC to S/4HANA). This process is technically independent from the migration of other postings, such as FI or CO postings. The advantages of this separate migration include: 

  • System Usability: The system can be used, and all kinds of postings can be performed even if the Accrual Engine has not yet been migrated. However, applications that rely on the Accrual Engine will not function until the migration is complete. 

 

  • Flexible Timing: The migration of the Accrual Engine can be scheduled at a different time, such as on a separate weekend. This is particularly useful if the initial migration activities are time-consuming and adding the Accrual Engine migration would exceed the available timeframe. 

 

  • Separate Tool: Technically, the migration of Accrual Engine transactional data uses a separate tool compared to the migration of other postings. It employs the mass data processing cockpit, which is an SAP standard tool for handling mass data processing. 

 

Migration of Accrual Engine Data for Customers Upgrading to S/4HANA 1809 

Customers upgrading from S/4HANA releases earlier than 1809 or from S/4HANA Finance to release 1809 are required to migrate the Accrual Engine data for Manual Accruals. Although these customers have already completed the conversion to S/4HANA, they may not typically revisit the IMG folder labeled "Conversion of Accounting to SAP S/4HANA." 

Error Message Notification 

To ensure that such customers are aware of the need to migrate the Accrual Engine, an error message will be triggered by the system whenever the old Accrual Engine is used for Manual Accruals (ACE_S4001).

entry.title: Planning with mode coupling(mode linkage) in discrte industry
entry.content:
Dear ePP/DS Gurus,

My business scenario for planning an scheduling is as follows.

Material X has 3 operations(Activitity P), for example 10, 20, 30, and each operation has alternative modes in PDS.

Operation(Activity P) : 10, Alternative Modes : AA1(Mode No 1), AA2(Mode No 2), AA3(Mode No 3)
Operation(Activity P) : 20, Alternative Modes : BB1(Mode No 1), BB2(Mode No 2)
Operation(Activity P) : 30, Alternative Modes : CC1(Mode No 1), CC2(Mode No 2), CC3(Mode No 3)
(Mode Linkage Type : Resource-Independent)

When it comes to scheduling, the relationships between Activities in my scenario are as follows.

AA1 or AA2 -> BB1 -> CC1 or CC2
AA3               -> BB2 -> CC3

I tried to use Resource Network for modeling the relationships but an error message, "Order could  ot be scheduled", is raised after executing SAP_PP_001 in Product View, which is the same symptoms in Note 3496388.

So I changed Mode No for each Mode with BAdi as follows.
Mode      Mode No
AA1          1
AA2          1
AA3          2
BB1          1
BB2          2
CC1          1
CC2          1
CC3          2

After changing Mode No, still left Material assigned with Resource Network, I executed Heuristic again. In this time, another error message is raised saying "Attempt was made to create two modes with the same number for a segment"

And found Note 1625010, 1625007, but these are not related with my scenario which is about MRP-based Detailed Schedulig.

Please guide me to reslove the problem and let me know how to configure for my scenario.

 

Thanks in advance for your cooperation. 

Best Regards,
Sean Lee

entry.title: Re: SAP UI5: ISSUE : Calling Deep Entity of JSON - SAP UI...
EWM Pack Specification: How to find the table/link between internal node GUID and Pack Spec ID?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am working with EWM Packaging Specifications in transaction /SCWM/PACKSPEC and I am trying to understand how the internal technical identifier stored in the below tables is related to the displayed Pack Spec ID on the screen of /SCWM/PACKSPEC.

Tables:

Header- /SCWM/S_PS_DL_HEADER
Content - /SCWM/S_PS_DL_CONTENT
Level - /SCWM/S_PS_DL_LEVEL
Level Element - /SCWM/S_PS_DL_LEVEL_ELEMENT

Basically, it is getting difficult to identify the Pack Specification created in /SCWM/PACKSPEC. When you open the tables to see the data, the Pack Spec ID is not stored in the tables, instead a GUID (Internal Node Number) is stored. So wanted to find a way which can give me a relation between them.

What I found so far:

  • The field PS_ID is shown in structure /SCWM/S_PS_HEADER_INT
  • Data element is /SCWM/DE_PSID
  • So this looks like a structure field, not a direct database table field

My doubt is:

Is there any standard SAP table that gives the relation between:

  • the internal node/header GUID (technical number, for example from pack spec tables), and
  • the normal Pack Spec ID (PS_ID) shown in /SCWM/PACKSPEC?

I am specifically looking for a table or standard object that can help convert/map:
internal GUID / node number → Pack Spec ID

If anyone knows:

  • the exact table name,
  • a CDS/view,
  • or the standard relationship between these objects,

that would really help.

 

Thank You

entry.title: Re: Joule Studio: Why is build_default required if standard plan exists?
entry.content: BTP entitlements are additive at the subaccount level. Assigning “build_default” does not remove or downgrade “standard,” and it will not affect running workflows, RPA bots, monitoring, or other capabilities already enabled by the “standard” plan. Best practice is to keep your “standard” entitlement for productive use and, if the activation requires it, add “build_default” to satisfy the Joule Studio checks.

entry.title: Re: Custom Validation not working for create incoming inv...
entry.content:
Dear @K12,

I reproduced your test cases in my own environment and below are my conclusions together with example code snippets and screenshots:

For Create Supplier Invoice app the correct enhancement spot is MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD
As a simple trigger I used field headerdata-UNPLANNEDDELIVERYCOST and checked whether it is initial. In my test the custom message was triggered correctly once the document context was complete

IF headerdata-UNPLANNEDDELIVERYCOST IS INITIAL.

  APPEND VALUE #(

    messagetype      = if_ex_mrm_check_invoice_cloud=>c_messagetype_warning

    messageid        = 'MRM_BADI_CLOUD'

    messagenumber    = '001'

    messagevariable1 = 'TEST'

  ) TO messages.

ENDIF.

For Create Supplier Invoice Advanced the same enhancement spot applies MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD BUT only for error NOT for warning based on information below, last text starting from Please note that….

I confirmed that also in my tenant

IF headerdata-UNPLANNEDDELIVERYCOST IS INITIAL.

  APPEND VALUE #(

    messagetype      = if_ex_mrm_check_invoice_cloud=>c_messagetype_error

    messageid        = 'MRM_BADI_CLOUD'

    messagenumber    = '001'

    messagevariable1 = 'TEST'

  ) TO messages.

ENDIF.

For Create Incoming Invoices the correct extension layer is different. SAP describes as the app used to create supplier invoices or credit memos without purchase order reference in Financial Accounting. Because of that the correct Badi is FIN_ACDOC_VALIDATION, not MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD

As a simple technical trigger I used header field accountingdocheader-AccountingDocumentHeaderText and checked whether it is initial. This worked on Post. SAP also explicitly documents that FIN_ACDOC_VALIDATION supports both Error and Warning messages and recommends standard journal entry validation rules for simpler header or item checks

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/1cbcff7ccd35405ab445b223c1ab1588/fed79813a60447669f096d9fb0713e16.html

IF accountingdocheader-AccountingDocumentHeaderText IS INITIAL.

  validationmessage-msgid = 'FIN_SUB_VAL'.

  validationmessage-msgty = 'W'.

  validationmessage-msgno = '000'.

  validationmessage-msgv1 = ‘TEST’.

  RETURN.

ENDIF.

 

 

Summary:

  • Create Supplier Invoice use MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD; warning worked
  • Create Supplier Invoice Advanced use MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD; error worked
  • Create Incoming Invoices (FB60) use FIN_ACDOC_VALIDATION; warning worked

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Missing index file at DART/GDPDU extract

Is there a known solution or a recommended approach for this? How exactly can we access the relevant index file? Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Navigating the depths of Data Aging in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
DimensionData AgingNSE (Native Storage Extension)Data Archiving / ILM
What it doesMoves data to cold HANA partition (still in DB, disk-resident)Offloads columns/tables to disk within HANA, transparent to applicationRemoves data from DB entirely; stores in archive files or ILM store
Data still in DB?YesYesNo
Memory reductionYes — cold partition not loaded into RAMYes — offloaded pages not in main memoryYes — data removed entirely
Disk space reductionNoNoYes
Backup size reductionNoNoYes
Custom code impactHigh — Z-programs querying aged tables silently return incomplete data unless temperature context is explicitly setNone — fully transparentMedium — depends on archiving info structures and access paths
Application coverageLimited — only SAP-delivered Data Aging Objects; custom objects require framework developmentAny table, including Z-tablesBroad — SAP ArchiveObjects for most application areas; ILM for retention-managed data
Regulatory / DPP complianceNo — data physically remains in DBNo — data physically remains in DBYes — supports blocking, deletion, and destruction under GDPR/local law
Reversible?Yes, but requires re-partitioningYes, easilyNo — retrieval is possible but not a rollback
Operational overheadHigh — partition management per landscape, non-transportable, aging runs to schedule/monitorLow — set and forget at column or table levelMedium — archive runs, retrieval config, ILM policy setup
Best forShort-term memory relief on application data with no immediate archiving pathTechnical/logging tables, large audit tables, Z-tables with historical dataPrimary long-term strategy for all application data; mandatory for DPP scenarios
RecommendationUse sparingly, as a tactical bridge — never as a permanent strategyPreferred over Data Aging for technical data; low risk, low effortDefault recommendation for all application data lifecycle management

entry.title: SAP SD Consultant role (OTC, S/4HANA)
entry.content:
Hello,
I am currently working in SAP SD as a Master Data specialist (customer data, pricing conditions, etc.), and I would like to transition into a functional SAP SD Consultant role (OTC, S/4HANA).

What practical skills am I still missing to be project-ready (customizing, pricing, FI/MM integration, debugging, etc.)?

Also, do you have any concrete advice or hands-on scenarios I could practice to make this transition more efficiently?

entry.title: Re: Batch Split Valuation in Product Cost Controlling and Margin Analysis
entry.content:
Hi @JavierIbanez  

Yes, for actual costing. the master master data needs to maintained as 3S, price determination 3 with standard cost.

but I'm not sure about your migration cockpit issue.

entry.title: Re: ORA-01792 (Database error 1792 at EXEC) in SAP ECC on...
entry.content:
Hi,

Lets start with the fact that Oracle 12.1 was supported for only a very-very short time and it is no longer supported for SAP Systems since July 2022. Following SAPs recommendation about the supported versions, one always have to install the latest Bundle Patch on a supported Oracle database release as soon as possible. Please check below SAP notes:
1174136 - Oracle: End of Support Dates

Considering these information, some of your question is no longer valid.

For "point 1" I don't really have a answer, but sure thing is that we have an SAP note that deals exactly with this error:
2413016 - ORA-1792 creating view CDS_DS_CCS

Do not ignore this error as it will later lead to further errors as soon as the related and dependent objects will be accessed by business processes. 
There are no risk to install the latest Bundle Patch if all is well prepared. Please test the Bundle Patch installation first on an identical system of your productive to avoid any issues later on with the real PROD system.

entry.title: Re: How to Retrieve PDFs from SAP ArchiveLink and embed them into the eDocument XML (SAP DRC)
entry.content: You’re right about ZUGFeRD in general – however, this blog post is not about ZUGFeRD. In my post, the XML acts as the technical container and includes a PDF, which is a different concept and not a ZUGFeRD implementation.

entry.title: Re: Collision Handling between Absences and Attendances in SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking - 1H 202
entry.content: amazing blog - thank you!

entry.title: Re: Time Valuation for 4 Cycle Shifts | SAP SuccessFactor...
entry.content:
Hi Parthu,

Thanks for your inputs. I have a concern though - if I switch to a 4-week period timesheet, I still need to run certain evaluations at a weekly level, specifically to check whether working hours in a given week exceed 40 hours. How can I handle that? Currently, I’m using “Valuate Whole Sheet or Period” with a weekly timesheet.

Also, I’ve noticed that Time Collectors no longer work in the Time Recording Profile once we move from a weekly timesheet to a custom duration. Regarding Time Containers, my understanding so far is that they can mainly be used to trigger timesheet alerts or to create another container with the same period results. However, when I try to use Time Container data to move results into a different Time Type Group - using “Aggregate Input Groups and Split” or “Route Input Based on Decision” - it doesn’t seem to work.

Have you come across a scenario where Time Containers were successfully used to move results into another Time Type Group for further payroll processing?

Thanks,
Saif

entry.title: Re: Batch Split Valuation in Product Cost Controlling and Margin Analysis
entry.content:
Hi Li_Sen,

Can we have batch number as characteristic in Margin analysis report without batch split/actual costing in private cloud 2023,

entry.title: Learning only users
entry.content:
Hi, I'm looking for a process / design which enables me to create accounts in Learning only for representatives of external training providers.  As these users are non-employee's, the process / design has to show how they are prevented from accessing any other elements of the SF Platform, particularly Employee Central. 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Change a GL account assigned to this activity
entry.content: did you solve thuis? i have the same issue. Thank you. Johannes

entry.title: Prerequisites for Creating Accrual Objects and Performing Accrual Postings
entry.content:
Before accrual objects can be created and accrual postings can be performed, specific Customizing settings must be maintained. These settings are used to define the following:

  • The types of accruals to be calculated and posted
  • The algorithms by which accruals will be calculated
  • The accrual accounts to be used during posting

The required Customizing activities can be accessed through the Accruals Management section of the Implementation Guide (IMG), either via:

  • Transaction ACEIMG, where the Accruals Management IMG tree is displayed directly
  • Transaction SPRO, by navigating through the IMG to the relevant node

 

The Customizing settings for Purchase Order Accruals are part of a separate application. These settings do not need to be configured if only manual accruals are to be used.

The Customizing settings required for Manual Accruals consist of two main components:

1. Settings for the Accrual Engine
Since the Manual Accruals application is built on the Accrual Engine, these settings must be configured first. They also serve as a prerequisite for the Customizing settings used in Purchase Order Accruals.

Important: The Accrual Engine is designed as a generic tool. Therefore, not all available settings need to be configured if the Accrual Engine is intended to be used only for Purchase Order Accruals.

 

2. Settings specific to the Manual Accruals application
These settings are used to define parameters and rules that apply only to the Manual Accruals functionality

entry.title: Efficient ABAP Query Design for Cleared Vendor and Customer Items (BKPF–BSAK / BKPF–BSAD)
entry.content:
Requirement Statement
We need to design efficient SELECT queries involving the following SAP tables:
  • BKPF (Accounting Document Header)
  • BSAK (Vendor Cleared Items)
  • BSAD (Customer Cleared Items)
Selection Screen Inputs
  • Mandatory fields:
    • Company Code (BUKRS)
    • Account Type (KOART)
    • Clearing Date (AUGDT)
    • Document Type (BLART)
    • Posting Key (BSCHL)
  • Optional fields:
    • Customer/Vendor Number (KUNNR / LIFNR)
    • Document Number (BELNR)
Challenge
  • In BKPF, the primary key requires BUKRS, BELNR, and GJAHR.
  • However, in the program:
    • Fiscal Year (GJAHR) is not available as input.
    • Document Number (BELNR) is optional.
We must therefore design queries that efficiently retrieve cleared items from BKPF + BSAK and BKPF + BSAD, while handling the absence of fiscal year input and optional document number.

entry.title: Re: The Hidden Surprise
entry.content:
Hi paolo,

Web client is calling the reporting service which is a java web service to execute a Crystal Report. In the reporting service it execute the CR via CR java runtime, which could be used cross platforms. The CR java runtime is different from the CR runtime which is used by B1 windows client.

Thanks,

Zhenyu

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit – Migrate Data Directly from SAP System – Using Staging Tables
entry.content:
Hello Philipp,

thanks a lot for your answer.

Unfortunately, I still haven’t been able to create the database connection with the correct attributes. Do you happen to have more concrete information about the technical DB user and its required properties/ accesses for this scenario?

Specifically:

  • What characteristics/authorizations should the DB user have for the DBCO connection used by the Migration Cockpit with staging tables as intermediate storage?
  • Are there any recommended minimum privileges (e.g. on schema level) or example roles for this user?

So far, the only information I find in the documentation is:

If you are using staging tables as intermediate storage, you need to create a database connection in transaction DBCO, and manually add it to table DMC_C_WL_DBCO_OP in the SAP S/4HANA system using transaction SM30.

Any concrete guidance or example configuration would be highly appreciated.

Thanks a lot,
Mario

entry.title: AI in Procurement - Office Hours: Upcoming Spend Management Value Map Events
entry.content:
In this blog post you will learn about upcoming Spend Management events on SAP Community, now featured in the Spend Management Value Map.

We’re introducing a new AI in Procurement Office Hour designed for practitioners who want practical conversations, not presentations. These are one‑day sessions happening in June, July, and September 2026, focused on how AI is being used today across procurement processes.

During each Office Hour, our Procurement AI experts will be available to:

  • Answer your questions in real time
  • Share practical adoption tips and lessons learned
  • Show how to get more value from AI-powered features in SAP Ariba and SAP Fieldglass

The sessions are interactive and built around what matters to you—how to apply AI in your daily work, where it fits in existing processes, and what to explore next.

You can read more details about the upcoming sessions and register here.

This is just the beginning. More events will continue to be populated under the Spend Management Value Map Events, so stay tuned and check back regularly to see what’s coming next.

Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps  

Beyond this topic, SAP Enterprise Support value maps on SAP Community provides guidance, recommended actions, and supporting resources to help you decide what to focus on next—all included in your SAP cloud subscription.  

 

👉Watch short demo video, then head over to our page - explore our range of topics and request a call with a topic expert for guidance tailored to your goals.   

About the author:  

Rachana is a Topic Expert for SAP Enterprise Support value maps, specializing in Spend Management. With over 15 years at SAP, she brings deep experience from a range of customer-facing roles. She enjoys working closely with customers to help them make the most of SAP Enterprise Support and drive real results.  

💡Have questions or feedback around this content? Don’t hesitate to comment down below or contact @Rachana_Rasania

entry.title: SAC setVisible via Switch
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am currently trying to set up my first true Story in SAC. I am using a live model with given Measures. I'd like to have a table that can dynamically change. Since I couldn't figure out how to change the used Measures and Dimensions in a table I tried setting up two seperate tables and toggleing their visibility with a switch. I tried various ways to make it work but since my programming knowladge is pretty low I just could not get it to run as I'd like to.

 Here you can see my code: 

var isSwitch2On = this.isOn();

if (isSwitch2On) {
		YTD_Tabelle.setVisible(false);
        Month_Tabelle.setVisible(true);
    } else {
        YTD_Tabelle.setVisible(false);
		Month_Tabelle.setVisible(true);
	}

I do not get any red likes or somthing and every variable is surely written correctly. However when I try to use the switch in view mode I always recieve a message simmilar to this one:

 

(Script execution failed for event "Switch_2.onChange" Correlation ID: 9553790471024494b717229046088729)

In the styling options I set the "View time visibility" to hidden for the Month_Tabelle and to visible for the YTD_Tabelle.

Thank you everyone for your help and time! 🙂

The first shows short infos and should link to a workpage with the other Component Card which should show the whole information set. But this workpage to show the whole information should not be visible in the navigation, because it should work as a generic template. When this page is listed in the navigation, everything works fine, if not or switched to hidden, I get "Access denied". Is it possible to get this working? 

I hope someone can help.

Thanks
Ralf

entry.title: Re: SAP SD Consultant role (OTC, S/4HANA)
entry.content:
You have not indicated as to how many years of SAP SD experience you have based on which, the advice can differ. 

You can simulate mini-projects in a sandbox system to build confidence and it would also be a practical hands-on scenarios to practice

1) Pricing Procedure Setup
Create a new pricing procedure for a specific sales org. Include discounts, surcharges, freight, and taxes. Test it end-to-end with a sales order and billing.

2) Order-to-Cash Flow
Configure a new sales document type (e.g., ZOR). Link it to delivery and billing types. Run through the full cycle and check FI postings.

3) Third-Party Order Scenario
Configure item category for third-party processing. Test integration with MM (purchase requisition creation).

4) Credit Management
Set up credit control area and risk categories. Test blocking/unblocking of sales orders.

5) Output Determination
Configure invoice outputs (PDF/email). Test condition records for different customers.

entry.title: AVC mass upload data into variant tables
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Since the CU60E isn’t available in the public cloud, I’m looking for a way to populate variant tables for variant configuration in bulk or via upload.

As far as i know the only way is manual entry or copying and pasting from Excel.

My client has quite extensive variant tables that he wants to maintain easily and efficiently.

entry.title: Re: mb5b and MB5w data miss match in TSP
entry.content:
Why MB5B and MB5W differ is because

1) Report Purpose

  • MB5B (Stock on Posting Date) - Shows stock values based on material documents and valuation at a specific posting date. It’s more of a financial view tied to accounting.
  • MB5W (Stock Valuation) - Shows stock values based on current inventory quantities and valuation class. It’s more of a logistics view tied to material management.

2) Valuation Method

  • MB5B uses actual postings (GR/IR, FI documents).
  • MB5W uses valuation class and moving average price or standard price. If prices changed after the posting date, MB5W will reflect the latest valuation, not the historical one.

3) Data Source

  • MB5B pulls from accounting tables (BKPF, BSEG). In S/4HANA, it would be ACDOCA 
  • MB5W pulls from material management tables (MBEW, MKPF, MSEG). Different sources → different logic.  In S/4HANA, it would be MATDOC 

Below would be the practical investigation approach

  • Run MB5B and MB5W for the same key date and same company code/materials.
  • Check valuation method (standard vs moving average price).
  • Compare FI postings vs MM stock quantities for a few materials.
  • Look for open GR/IR balances — MB5B will show them, MB5W may not.
  • Check if MB5W is run with “as of date” — if not, it shows current values, not historical.

Having said that, my advice would be

  • Use MB5B when reconciling with FI (financial reporting).
  • Use MB5W when reconciling with MM (inventory management).
  • For reconciliation projects, many companies use MB5L (List of Stock Values), which is designed to bridge FI and MM differences.

entry.title: OData V4 Fiori Elements: "KeyPath missing at p.getItemFromContext" when selecting Value Help - Fiori
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I am facing a persistent UI5 ERROR in an OData V4 Fiori Elements application (based on ABAP RAP).

The Scenario: I have a Root Consumption View with an input Parameter. I have assigned a custom CDS Value Help (Dropdown / #XS) to this parameter. The dropdown successfully opens and displays the data in the Fiori Preview.

The Error: When the user clicks/selects an item from the dropdown list, the slection doesn't happen with the following console error in the browser:

Uncaught (in promise) Error: KeyPath missing at p.getItemFromContext

1. Value Help CDS View (Single Key, Dropdown)

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Value Help for Parameter'
@ObjectModel.resultSet.sizeCategory: #XS  

define view entity ZI_VH_CustomValue
  as select from custom_domain_table
{
      @UI.lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
        key cast( domain_value as abap.char(1) ) as ValueKey,

      @UI.lineItem: [{ position: 20 }]
      @Semantics.text: true
      domain_text as Description
}
where active_status = 'A'

2. The Consumption View (Short Version)

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Main Business Object'
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true

define root view entity ZC_MainBusinessObject
  with parameters

    @EndUserText.label: 'Custom Filter Parameter'
    @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{
        entity: { name: 'ZI_VH_CustomValue', element: 'ValueKey' },

        /* Explicit binding to pass the value back to the parameter */
        additionalBinding: [{
            localParameter: 'p_custom_filter',
            element: 'ValueKey',
            usage: #RESULT
        }]
    }]
    p_custom_filter : z_custom_data_element

  as select from ZI_MainBusinessObject
{
  key DocumentId,
      DocumentType,
      PostingDate
}
where PostingDate <= $session.system_date

My Questions:

  1. Is there a known limitation or missing @ui/ @Consumption annotation required to bind a Value Help strictly to a PARAMETER (rather than a standard table field) in OData V4?
  2. Why is the OData V4 framework failing to resolve the context for the selected item despite the explicit localParameter binding?

Any insights or workarounds would be highly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Cancel the GRN document post UD Clearance in SAP Public cloud.
entry.content:
Hi Team,

User has performed the GRN and  also cleared the quality posting as well, now the post receiving the material into stock w.r.t Purchase order. they come to know that they have received the wrong product. Kindly suggest how to reverse the GRN Entry and again perform GRN w.r.t same Purchase order. So that the required action could be performed again. 

Below error is coming while performing the Cancellation :

You cannot cancel GR, since inspection lot is already partly posted

Message no. QA046

Diagnosis

You are attempting to reverse a goods receipt although quantity postings have already been made from the inspection lot. This means that stock for the goods receipt has already been transferred and, for this reason, a cancelation of the document is no longer possible.

 

Procedure

Ensure that the postings in QM (within the usage decision transactions) are carried out.

entry.title: Re: Issue with DME file
entry.content: Please refer to SAP KBA 2846873 and 3507331 for your query

entry.title: Re: Material Deletion Error
entry.content: Deletion flag ≠ hard block. Use material status (blocked for sales) to enforce that the material cannot be used in sales orders at all. Go to material master Sales Org.1 view tab and set some block against "X-distr.chain status". Try to create a new sales order and see how it works

Please allow me a bit more time to make that happen.

entry.title: After deleting tiles catalog and group, and subsequently performing role synchronization in PFCG
entry.content:
We have removed the tile FS00 from the custom Fiori catalog  ZF_FI_D_FINANCIAL_MANAGER_TRV. After deleting the tile from the catalog (and associated group)

However, when we perform role synchronization in PFCG, we observe that multiple application names are proposed again for addition in the role, including applications related to the previously deleted tile. Along with these newly added application entries

We request clarification on whether this behavior is standard by design in SAP Fiori Launchpad and guidance on the correct

entry.title: Re: Wrong due date calculation
entry.content: Please refer to SAP KBA 3340341

entry.title: Re: Cancel the GRN document post UD Clearance in SAP Publ...

entry.title: Line item text in different lengeage in different leders
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

In our company we use 2 ledgers - 0L and ZL. For SAF-T  file for Poland we need to porvide line item text in Polish in ZL but in 0L we must keep English text. Text in Polish must be Description of GL in Polish+document type in Polish+RefNr.

Is it possible? and If yes, please help me with a solution.

entry.title: Re: SAC setVisible via Switch
entry.content:
Does the below work ? Your true and else condition look same in terms of table being shown and hidden.

if (Switch_1.isOn()) // Switch_1 is the toggle switch
{
		YTD_Tabelle.setVisible(false);
        Month_Tabelle.setVisible(true);
    } else {
        YTD_Tabelle.setVisible(true);
		Month_Tabelle.setVisible(false);
	}

Nikhil

entry.title: Manage Batches Fiori APP F2462 not support for digital signature
entry.content: Manage Batches Fiori APP F2462 not support for digital signature , as same support to Change batch app.

entry.title: SAP CPQ: How i can reload the "My Quote" Tab on triggering custom Action button on Approval page
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have created a custom action button called Approve on the Approvals Page using the responsive template. This button successfully triggers the quote approval to the next level.

However, after clicking the custom Approve  button, the quote is submitted to the next approval level, but the page does not redirect or reload to sections like My Quotes or Waiting for Approval.

In contrast, when using the standard Approve button, once the quote is approved, the system automatically redirects the user back to the My Quotes section.

Could you please suggest how we can achieve similar page reload or redirection behavior using the custom button?

great post, somwthing like this I´ve been searching for long time.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: SAC setVisible via Switch
entry.content: Thank you for your reply, Nikhil. Sadly, I am still receiving the same error message.

entry.title: Re: Action Required: Update your certificate trust stores for enhanced SAP HANA Cloud security
entry.content:
• DigiCert G5 migration only affects HANA Cloud public endpoints — If the customer's system already trusts "DigiCert RSA4096 Root G5", that's sufficient — no need to also trust the older G2 chain for that connection  
  • SAP Cloud Root CA is a completely independent certificate issued by SAP itself for other SAP service endpoints — it is not impacted by this G5 migration However, if a customer's system connects to both HANA Cloud and other SAP services, they need to trust both certificates in their trust store — not because of the G5 migration, but because they are separate trust chains for separate services

entry.title: Re: SAP S4HANA FI New exam pattern
entry.content:
Hi

 

Complete Learning Journey, https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implementing-financial-accounting-in-sap-s-4hana

The 2026 certification framework emphasizes Business Process Integration. This means questions rarely stay within the "FI" silo; they will test how FI interacts with MM (Materials Management) and SD (Sales and Distribution).

Instead of a simple multiple-choice question, you might encounter a "Case Study" block. You are given a business requirement (e.g., "Company X is expanding to a new country with specific VAT requirements") and must select the sequence of configuration steps from a list.

While creating staging warehouse task in -/SCWM/STAGE

There are 5 components in production order that needs to be stage

So businesses expectation is that it should create WT for components with available stock qty

 

Thanks,

Virsinh Jadhav

entry.title: Call transaction CS02 ignores AEN (AENNR) field
entry.content:
Hi SAP gurus,

I'm having a weird problem that I cannot solve.

I have an ABAP program that displays an ALV grid with various info regarding a material. When the user clicks on the BOM cells of the ALV grid, one of my methods calls: CALL TRANSACTION 'CS02' AND SKIP FIRST SCREEN.

My code states:

SET PARAMETER ID 'AEN' FIELD lv_datum_fmt.

Where lv_datum_fmt is:

DATA: lv_datum_fmt TYPE char8.
lv_datum_fmt = sy-datum+6(2)
            && sy-datum+4(2)
            && sy-datum+0(4).
SHIFT lv_datum_fmt LEFT DELETING LEADING '0'.

The weird part is this: I use basically an identical method to call transaction CA02, and AEN is properly set up and the transaction opens correctly. But in CS02, the AEN is always initial and the transaction opens with a warning that the AENNR is not set up.

Can you please help me understand what I'm doing wrong? Is it perhaps a bug that has been fixed with a note?

Regards

entry.title: Give Your Joule Agents Escrow-Protected Commerce with Synmerco MCP Server (29 Tools)
entry.content:
Your Joule agent is excellent at what it does inside SAP. But what happens when it needs to hire an external specialist — a code auditor, a market researcher, a data analyst — and pay them?

Your procurement and compliance teams will never approve it. No escrow. No insurance. No audit trail. No reputation verification. No dispute resolution. The liability is unlimited and unmanaged.

Synmerco is the missing trust layer. Escrow-protected payments, $1K insurance, on-chain reputation (ERC-8004), and automated dispute resolution — so your Joule agents can safely transact with any of 172,000+ external agents. We just launched a remote MCP server your Joule agents can connect to in 5 minutes via a BTP Destination.

29 MCP Tools for Agent Commerce

Discovery (free, no auth):
- lookup_trust_score — verify any agent's reputation before engaging
- search_agents — find agents by capability and minimum trust score
- estimate_fees — transparent fee calculation (3.25% flat)
- get_platform_info — platform documentation and chain details

Commerce:
- create_escrow — funds locked until work is verified by buyer
- fund_escrow, start_work, submit_proof, release_escrow — full lifecycle
- post_job — post work to a FREE marketplace of 172K+ agents
- start_negotiation / counter_offer — structured price negotiation protocol

Every transaction includes:
- $1,000 Shield Insurance — included in fee, no extra cost
- On-chain reputation (ERC-8004) on Base, Arbitrum, Polygon, Optimism
- Confidential Escrow Mode for HIPAA/GDPR workloads (end-to-end encrypted, Synmerco never sees content)
- 3-tier automated dispute resolution with precedent tracking
- Hash-chained audit trail for compliance
- Spending limits and budget controls for enterprise governance

Setup via BTP Destination (5 Minutes)

Step 1: Get a free API key

POST https://synmerco-escrow.onrender.com/v1/onboard
Content-Type: application/json
{"did": "did:web:your-company.sap.com"}

One call. Returns your API key, wallet, and agent profile.

Step 2: Create a BTP Destination

In SAP BTP Cockpit, go to Connectivity, then Destinations, then New Destination:

Name: Synmerco_MCP
Type: HTTP
URL: https://synmerco-escrow.onrender.com/mcp
Proxy Type: Internet
Authentication: NoAuthentication

Additional Properties:
sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.mcp.enabled = true
sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.mcp.transport = streamable-http

Step 3: Connect in Joule Studio

1. Open your agent in Joule Studio
2. Go to Tools, then Add MCP Server
3. Select the Synmerco_MCP destination
4. Joule auto-discovers all 29 tools

Step 4: Test

Ask your Joule agent: "Look up the trust score for did🔑zExample123"

Use Cases for SAP

Procurement: Your procurement agent needs a specialized market research report for a sourcing decision. It posts the job on Synmerco, finds a qualified agent with a verified trust score, escrows payment until the report meets quality standards. Full audit trail for procurement compliance.

Supply Chain: Your logistics optimization agent needs real-time analysis from an external specialist. It negotiates price through Synmerco's structured protocol, escrows payment, and pays on delivery. Every step logged.

Finance: Your accounts payable agent needs invoice verification from a third-party auditor. It uses Confidential Escrow Mode — the financial data stays encrypted throughout. Synmerco never sees the content. HIPAA/GDPR compliant.

A2A Protocol Support

Synmerco also supports Google's A2A protocol for agent-to-agent task delegation. Your Joule agent can use both MCP (for tool access) and A2A (for task delegation) simultaneously:
https://synmerco.com/.well-known/agent.json

Pricing

3.25% flat fee per released escrow. No monthly fees. No minimums. Free discovery tools work without any authentication.

Resources

- Live MCP Endpoint: POST https://synmerco-escrow.onrender.com/mcp
- Full Integration Guide: https://synmerco.com/sap-joule-integration.md
- Ready-to-paste Configs: https://synmerco.com/enterprise-mcp-configs.json
- API Docs: https://synmerco.com/docs
- Python SDK: pip install synmerco (42 methods, LangChain compatible)
- MCP Discovery: https://synmerco.com/.well-known/mcp.json
- Quick Start: https://synmerco.com/quickstart.html

The endpoint is live right now — 29 tools, Streamable HTTP. Test the free tools first, no commitment needed. Questions? Drop them in the comments.

entry.title: SAC Story embedded in Fiori LP - Jump to... not working for hierarchies
entry.content:
Hi All,

we are trying to setup SAC Stories based on S4 CDS views. For us it is crucial that the SAC Stories are embedded in Fiori Launchpad and that the integration into other Fiori Apps as e.g. Actual Line Items works smoothly. We have a SAC Story embedded, but the G/L Account and Cost Center hierarchies are not resolved as soon as we jump into a Line Item Fiori App.

Display Line Items in General Ledger window is empty.
Anyone has a solution for that?

entry.title: Re: Impossible to copy/paste an object, from one page of...
entry.content:
Hi @Alex_Unige_2024,

There are some restrictions when copying/pasting objects, and this chapter in the Product Help guide gives you a clear explanation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/122dd3bf9c3a466593f5344d9f22a89f.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST

It mentions that in the Optimized Story, Calculation input controls cannot be copied/pasted.

For why you cannot recreate it on the 3rd page, I need more context around the steps you performed.

entry.title: Re: Week 3 - Use AI Agents with Grounding Tools
entry.content:
Hi 
I just saw this challenge, Can I start from Week 1 and complete the challenege to catch up.
Kind Regards,

Suresh

entry.title: Issue in Tyre Equipment Installation, Plant Synchronization, and Time Segment Data in SAP S/4hPublic
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We are facing multiple issues in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (EAM – Plant Maintenance) related to tyre management using serialized equipment.

🔎Business Scenario

  • Tyre is maintained as a stockable serialized material
  • Equipment is automatically created from serial number (Equipment Category: X)
  • Tyres are installed on a Superior Equipment (Vehicle – Equipment Category: M)

❗Issues Faced

  1. Plant Not Updating for Child Equipment
    • When we change the Superior Equipment (Vehicle) plant from 1100 to 1200, the child equipment (Tyre) plant remains 1100
    • Expectation: Child equipment should align or system should guide correct process
  1. Installation / Dismantle Date Not Updating
    • Installation and dismantle activities are performed
    • However:
      • Valid From / Valid To dates are not updating correctly
      • CDS views (like I_EquipmentTimeSegment) are not reflecting proper data

🎯Business Requirement

We want to achieve the following process:

  1. GRN of Tyre (Serialized Material)
    • Equipment should be auto-created
  2. Activate Equipment View
    • Equipment should be ready for installation
  3. Install Tyre on Vehicle
    • Proper installation date capture
  4. Dismantle Tyre
    • Proper dismantle date capture
  5. Tracking Requirement
    • System should track:
      • Tyre lifecycle
      • Installation history
      • Running (usage) of tyre on each vehicle
  6. Plant Transfer Scenario
    • When vehicle (superior equipment) moves plant:
      • Either child equipment should update
      • OR system should enforce correct process (stock transfer + reinstall)

❓Clarifications Required

  1. Is the current behavior (no plant sync between superior and child equipment) standard in Public Cloud?
  2. What is the recommended process for:
    • Moving serialized equipment across plants?
  3. Why are Valid From / To dates not updating during install/dismantle?
  4. Any configuration required to ensure: 
    • Proper update of I_EquipmentTimeSegment CDS View?
  5. Best practice to track tyre running per vehicle in Public Cloud?

 

Regards,
Mohit Mishra

entry.title: Key Points for SAP GRC for SAP HANA (SAP GRC 2026) Upgrade
entry.content:
Take a look at the high-level points below for "SAP GRC for SAP HANA" (SAP GRC 2026) upgrade:

1. Software components to be installed : SAPGRC, GRCPIS4 (HR), GRCPIBAS (non-HR), UISAPGRC.
2. SAP NetWeaver should be minimum SAP S/4HANA Foundation
3. NWBC is replaced by Fiori Apps
4. HANA DB is mandatory
5. System needs to be a Unicode
6. No minimum SP level prerequisite for upgrade
7. SAP recommends the embedded deployment option for SAP Fiori UI for SAP GRC 2026 (SAP GRC for SAP HANA 1.0)
8. For a one-step Upgrade, system needs to have SAP Access Control 12.0. The Upgrade to SAP GRC 2026 (SAP GRC for SAP HANA) is based on the de-installation of the ABAP artifacts and table entries of  SAP Access Control 12.0 and subsequent installation of the new product. All the existing data and configurations are kept during this process.
9. For technical upgrade of the system, use Software Update Manager SUM 2.0 SP023 or a later version, use Support Package Manager SPAM Release 92 or a later version & the Add-On ST-PI 740 should have support package level 20 or higher.
10. Zero Downtime Option (ZDO) is not supported for this upgrade.
11. SAP GRC for SAP HANA uses the special feature for upgrade with combined uninstallation and installation, as the main software component changes from GRCFND_A to SAPGRC. The plugin GRCPINW changes to GRCPIBAS and GRCPIERP to GRCPIS4.
12. Depending on the server target release, the plug-in may need to be upgraded or uninstalled/installed using SUM (Software Update Manager). Use Correction of Installed Software Information (CISI) in Maintenance Planner to correct or update installed product and product instance information when inconsistencies exist in the system landscape.
13. SPAU/SPDD required for custom changes when using SUM or SUM DMO.
14. Review the SAP NOTEs for upgrade: 3709892, 3677094, 3677114, 3677113, 3677122, 3677095, 3669604, 3699764, 3634546, 3574267, 3568504, 070228, 83458.

Refer the help portal link for more details: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_GRC_ACCESS_CONTROL_FOR_SAP_HANA

entry.title: The line item violates the cutoff value of -12.40 in area 01 Message No. AA632
entry.content:
Dear Sir/Madam,

Getting below error while settling WBS DR901-000002-11 in CJ88 

The line item violates the cut off value of -12.40 in area 01

Message No. AA632

PL. see the attachment and suggest the solution as our connection is already opened..

Regards,

Dr. Dharmveer Vishwakarma

Cell-9903327581

entry.title: Re: Continuous Feedback Details Delete Button
entry.content: @Yilin- What the KBA is describing above is a little different than my issue. This describes the button is still available after selecting Disable Deleting Feedback in Configurations. My issue is the Disable Deleting Feedback is no longer available to select in the configurations.

entry.title: fagll03 is very slow on fagl_get_items_faglbsas
entry.content:
With a specific selection on FAGLL03 there are 17 entries in table FAGLBSAS.

The system gets this information through function FAGL_GET_ITEMS_FAGLBSAS.

Other accounts in this table have only 4 or 6 entries and are very quickly.

The difference is that the faster run finds all data through the command OPEN CURSOR while the 17 entries are not immediately covered by that and need an extra step: FETCH NEXT CURSOR.

This FETCH NEXT CURSOR is the step that takes a very long time. I can see that it does an inner-join between FAGLBSAS and FAGLFLEXA but if I do that myself, it's very fast (despite more than 40 million entries in FAGLFLEXA).

Is there any way how this can be solved ?

Kind regards,

Rene

entry.title: SAC Scripting Function Top N Filter
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

for my story I implemented a dynamic measure change via a switch for three of my charts. One of them is as standard equipped with a top n filter. However, when i flip the switch and the new measures are shown the filter is being removed.

Do you know if there is a function to enable the filter again afterwards?

Thank you all in advance!

var measuresStandard2 = ["", "", "", ""];
var measuresAlternative2 = ["", "", "", ""];

var measuresStandard3 = [""];
var measuresAlternative3 = [""];

var isSwitchOn = this.isOn();

if (isSwitchOn) {
    // Chart 1
    for (var i = 0; i < measuresStandard1.length; i++) { Chart_1.removeMeasure(measuresStandard1[i], Feed.ValueAxis); }
    for (var j = 0; j < measuresAlternative1.length; j++) { Chart_1.addMeasure(measuresAlternative1[j], Feed.ValueAxis); }

    // Chart 2
    for (var k = 0; k < measuresStandard2.length; k++) { Chart_2.removeMeasure(measuresStandard2[k], Feed.ValueAxis); }
    for (var l = 0; l < measuresAlternative2.length; l++) { Chart_2.addMeasure(measuresAlternative2[l], Feed.ValueAxis); }

    // Chart 3
    for (var m3 = 0; m3 < measuresStandard3.length; m3++) { Chart_3.removeMeasure(measuresStandard3[m3], Feed.ValueAxis); }
    for (var n3 = 0; n3 < measuresAlternative3.length; n3++) { Chart_3.addMeasure(measuresAlternative3[n3], Feed.ValueAxis); }

} else {
    // Chart 1
    for (var m = 0; m < measuresAlternative1.length; m++) { Chart_1.removeMeasure(measuresAlternative1[m], Feed.ValueAxis); }
    for (var n = 0; n < measuresStandard1.length; n++) { Chart_1.addMeasure(measuresStandard1[n], Feed.ValueAxis); }

    // Chart 2
    for (var o = 0; o < measuresAlternative2.length; o++) { Chart_2.removeMeasure(measuresAlternative2[o], Feed.ValueAxis); }
    for (var p = 0; p < measuresStandard2.length; p++) { Chart_2.addMeasure(measuresStandard2[p], Feed.ValueAxis); }

    // Chart 3
    for (var o3 = 0; o3 < measuresAlternative3.length; o3++) { Chart_3.removeMeasure(measuresAlternative3[o3], Feed.ValueAxis); }
    for (var p3 = 0; p3 < measuresStandard3.length; p3++) { Chart_3.addMeasure(measuresStandard3[p3], Feed.ValueAxis); }
}

entry.title: Re: The line item violates the cutoff value of -12.40 in...
entry.content: SAP note 3662789 provides solution to your issue

entry.title: Re: How to merge multiple PREQ into 1 PREQ or 1purchasing order?
entry.content: I don´t know how long this had been released, but this is possible through specific transacions. Please check note below: https://me.sap.com/notes/2993892

entry.title: Maintenance Scheduling Board: people not showing up for Operation assignment
entry.content:
Hi all,

I can't assign Operation to people of a Team because they do not appear after clicking "Assign People" button:

 

I'm getting crazy becasue I have created a Maintenance Work Center, I have assigned some people to this Work Center, I have created a Team, all people have "Authorized" status, but I can't to understand how they do not appear.

Thank you so much for your help.

Alessandro

entry.title: Re: AVC mass upload data into variant tables
entry.content: In Public Cloud, manual entry or Excel paste is the standard, but for large-scale or recurring updates, you will need to leverage Data Migration Cockpit or build a custom upload solution.

entry.title: Keep the later approver in the route map for Compensation when wanting to remove duplicate approvers
entry.content:
In our compensation cycles we have approvers who hold two roles - this year we were able to remove these duplicate approvals.

However is it possible to keep the later approver step and remove the initial one? I could see no option for this in the route map itself.

entry.title: Re: AVC mass upload data into variant tables
entry.content:
Dear @DKN1,

In Public Cloud this requirement should be positioned as not covered by a standard CU60E-style upload transaction/app for recurring AVC variant table maintenance. In the current SAP Public Cloud documentation, SAP positions Migration Cockpit for the initial migration side and Variant Table Content API operations for mass maintenance of table content

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/20607b6de19a4d34a41b0abec6186b01.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/7489fa08cede494cbdf08fa3651598af/07f414a58cf1494ea6c7c1c1d1489f63.html

So for migration, SAP states that migration objects in Public Cloud are intended for initial migration and the relevant AVC migration object listed is AVC Variant table structure. That supports the setup/load phase, not an ongoing business-user upload process for table entries

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/d9edb38484e54273a4912647c5b8e506.html

For ongoing maintenance, SAP documents Variant Table Content operations such as CreateLine, DeleteLine, DeleteAllLines and RetrieveByKeyDate and explicitly supports using a BATCH request / change set to process multiple updates together. That is the SAP-backed mass-maintenance mechanism for variant table content in Public Cloud

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/7489fa08cede494cbdf08fa3651598af/07f414a58cf1494ea6c7c1c1d1489f63.html

If your client needs efficient maintenance of large variant tables the clean Public Cloud approach is to use an Excel/CSV-to-API solution instead of manual UI maintenance. In practice that means a small uploader on SAP BTP, Integration Suite or another external integration layer that reads the spreadsheet and sends batched calls to the documented Variant Table Content API

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Estimated Cost should not be released if BOM is not d...
entry.content: In Public Cloud, you cannot freely create new costing variants, but you can work with the delivered ones and adjust their settings. The key is the valuation strategy sequence inside the costing variant. By removing fallback options such as “material master price” or “purchasing info record,” and leaving only BOM explosion in the sequence, the system will require a BOM for costing. If a BOM is missing, the costing run will stop with an error instead of releasing a cost estimate.
